content.full_text:
State of California
Fair Political Practices Commission
P.O. BOX 807 • SACRAMENTO, 95804 • • • 1100 K STREET BUILDING, SACRAMENTO, 95814

Technical Assistance • • Administration • • Executive/Legal • • Enforcement
(916) 322-5662 322-5660 322-5901 322-5441

May 23, 1984

John R. Shaw
City Attorney
City of Pittsburg
P.O. Box 1518
Pittsburg, CA 94565

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our No. A-84-105

Dear Mr. Shaw:

Your letter requests advice on behalf of Joseph Silno, former Mayor and Councilmember of the City of Pittsburg. You have succinctly stated the facts in your letter as follows:

FACTS

The facts are that the City of Pittsburg entered into a concessionaire agreement with Dona Batten, on or about July 6 1981. At that time, when the City Council voted to approve the subject concessionaire agreement ... Joseph Silno, as Mayor, not only voted for the concessionaire agreement, but also signed it as Mayor.

The concessionaire agreement provides in short that the City will make available certain city-owned land to concessionaire for the purpose of allowing the construction of a waterside facility....

On or about March 16, 1982, the agreement was amended to allow for the substitution of Dona Batten as concessionaire by a newly formed corporation namely, "Ramblin Rivers." Councilman Silno voted for this substitution.

John R. Shaw
May 23, 1984
Page 2


At present, Dona Batten is the sole shareholder of
the corporation.

Former Councilman Joseph Sino, in conjunction
with five other individuals, have now proposed to
become co-shareholders in Ramblin Rivers Corporation.
Six individuals would provide infusion of money that
would provide financing for the construction of the
waterslide facility. These six individuals together
with Dona Batten would become equal shareholders in the
corporation.

Former Councilman Sino has now queried whether
his investment in the waterslide facility as a
co-shareholder in the corporation at this time would
constitute a conflict of interest under Government Code
Sections 87100 et. seq.

Former Councilman Sino represents that at the
time of voting on the concessionaire agreement, he did
not have any intent ... to participate in any way in
the future construction, funding or investment in the
waterslide facility, nor at any time since July 6, 1981
has he acquired any interest in the concessionaire's
assets.

ANALYSIS

Since Mr. Sino had no intention of becoming involved in the
concession at the time of his votes on the contract, there was
no basis then for a conflict of interest. Now that he is no
longer a public official, he is free (as regards Chapter 7 of
the Political Reform Act/) to pursue his private financial
interests.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91014. Chapter 7
commences with Section 87100. We express no opinion on the
possible applicability of Government Code Sections 1090,
et seq. As a precautionary step, Mr. Sino may wish to check
with the Attorney General's office regarding these provisions of
law. See Stigall v. City of Taft (9162) 58 Cal. 2d 565.

John R. Shaw
May 23, 1984
Page 3


I trust that this satisfactorily responds to your request
for advice. Should you have any questions regarding this
letter, I may be reached at (916) 322/5901.

Sincerely,


Robert E. Leidigh
Counsel
Legal Division

REL:plh

.

.                    .

OFFICE OF

THE CITY ATTORNEY
City of Pittsburg

JOHN R. SHAW
CITY ATTORNEY

P.O. BOX 1518
2020 RAILROAD AVENUE
PITTSBURG, CALIFORNIA 94565
(415) 439-0800

April 24, 1984

Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street - Second Floor
Sacramento, California 95814

Attention: Legal Department

Re: Request for an Informal Opinion

This letter is to request an informal opinion on behalf
of former Councilman Joseph Sino who recently vacated
office on February 15, 1984.

Former Councilman Sino has requested an informal
opinion as to whether there would be any conflict of interest
under Government Code § 87100 et. seq., in the event he
purchased stock in a corporation owning concessionaire
rights with the City of Pittsburg.

The facts are that the City of Pittsburg entered into a
concessionaire agreement with Dona Batten, on or about July 6
1981. At that time, when the City Council voted to approve
the subject concessionaire agreement (copy enclosed) Joseph
Sino, as Mayor, not only voted for the concessionaire
agreement, but also signed it as Mayor.

The concessionaire agreement provides in short that the
City will make available certain city-owned land to con-
cessionaire for the purpose of allowing the construction of
a waterslide facility. The terms and conditions of the
agreement are set forth in Exhibit A.

On or about March 16, 1982, the agreement was amended
to allow for the substitution of Dona Batten as concessionaire
by a newly formed corporation namely, "Ramblin Rivers."
(See Exhibit B) Councilman Sino voted for this substitution.

At present, Dona Batten is the sole shareholder of the
corporation.

Former Councilman Joseph Sino, in conjunction with
five other individuals, have now proposed to become co-
shareholders in Ramblin Rivers Corporation. Six individuals
would provide infusion of money that would provide financing
for the construction of the waterslide facility. These six
individuals together with Dona Batten would become equal
shareholders in the corporation.

Fair Political Practices Commission
Attention: Legal Department
April 24, 1984
Page Two

Former Councilman Siino has now queried whether his
investment in the waterside facility as a co-shareholder in
the corporation at this time would constitute a conflict of
interest under Government Code §§ 87100 et. seq.

Former Councilman Siino represents that at the time of
voting on the concessionaire agreement, he did not have any
intent at that time to participate in any way in the future
construction, funding or investment in the waterside
facility, nor at any time since July 6, 1981 has he acquired
any interest in the concessionaire's assets.

Former Councilman Siino would appreciate a response as
quickly as possible as the investment group would like to
make a decision soon as to whether to enter into the waterside
investment.

If you should need any additional information, please
advise.

Your cooperation is most appreciated.

Very truly yours,

[signature]
JOHN R. SHAW

JRS/dk

attachments

cc: Joseph Siino w/o attachments

sections.question: Whether his investment in the waterslide facility as a co-shareholder in the corporation at this time would constitute a conflict of interest under Government Code Sections 87100 et. seq.
sections.conclusion: Since Mr. Siino had no intention of becoming involved in the concession at the time of his votes on the contract, there was no basis then for a conflict of interest. Now that he is no longer a public official he is free (as regards Chapter 7 of the Political Reform Act) to pursue his private financial interests. We express no opinion on the possible applicability of Government Code Sections 1090, et seq. As a precautionary step, Mr. Siino may wish to check with the Attorney General's office regarding these provisions of law.

sections.question_synthetic: Whether a former city councilmember who previously voted to approve and amend a concessionaire agreement for a waterslide facility may now invest as a co-shareholder in the corporation operating that facility without violating conflict of interest laws.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concludes that because Mr. Siino had no intent to participate in the waterslide facility when he voted on the original agreement, there was no conflict of interest at that time. Now that he is no longer a public official, he is generally free to pursue the investment under Chapter 7 of the Political Reform Act, though he should consult the Attorney General regarding other applicable state law provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: modern; LLM: Document includes both the incoming request letter (April 24, 1984) and the FPPC response letter (May 23, 1984). The response letter clearly identifies this as advice letter A-84-105. The FPPC explicitly declines to opine on Government Code Sections 1090 et seq. and recommends consultation with the Attorney General's office.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether his investment in the waterslide facility as a co-shareholder in the corporation at this time would constitute a conflict of interest under Government Code Sections 87100 et. seq.

CONCLUSION: Since Mr. Siino had no intention of becoming involved in the concession at the time of his votes on the contract, there was no basis then for a conflict of interest. Now that he is no longer a public official he is free (as regards Chapter 7 of the Political Reform Act) to pursue his private financial interests. We express no opinion on the possible applicability of Government Code Sections 1090, et seq. As a precautionary step, Mr. Siino may wish to check with the Attorney General's office regarding these provisions of law.

embedding.summary: This is an FPPC advice letter responding to a request from the City Attorney of Pittsburg on behalf of former Councilman Joseph Siino, who seeks confirmation that investing as a co-shareholder in a waterslide facility corporation would not violate conflict of interest laws, given his prior votes to approve the initial concessionaire agreement.
